response.justification: Bogdan presents a genuinely relevant profile for parts of this role: current Staff-level work on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling at re:cinq maps directly to the AI-native engineering environment, and prior experience at Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests per second demonstrates credible high-throughput distributed-systems depth. However, Python — an explicit must-have — is entirely absent from the resume, skills list, and all employment history. The distributed-systems work is also now six-plus years old, the current role is too recent (April 2025) to validate sustained impact, and the resume provides no evidence of cross-functional stakeholder management, on-call operational ownership, or incident-response capability.
strengths: Current hands-on work building AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generators at re:cinq directly satisfies the AI-native engineering environment baseline the role treats as non-negotiable.

Verifiable high-throughput distributed-systems experience at Sociomantic — millions of HTTP req/s, async I/O, low-latency microservices — demonstrates the architectural depth and systems-thinking breadth the role demands.
Consistent Staff-level scope across multiple employers, strong TypeScript and Node.js fluency, and a well-documented TDD and agile practice spanning several years and teams.
concerns: Python is listed as a must-have technical skill but is completely absent from every section of the resume, including the explicit skills inventory.
The most recent distributed-systems work at scale (Sociomantic) ended in 2020; the current role began April 2025 and is too new to demonstrate sustained impact at Staff level.
No evidence anywhere in the resume of cross-functional stakeholder management, incident response, or operational on-call ownership — all listed as must-have non-technical skills.
